outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Trump ties himself in knots to avoid resuming a full-scale war in Iran as Beijing summit opens
outlets.summary: Analysis: Trump simultaneously calls ceasefire 'on life support,' rejects Iran's proposal as 'garbage,' then boards Air Force One for Beijing claiming 'I don't need Xi's help.'

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Grocery prices spiked highest since Iran war began, as April CPI data confirms food inflation surge
outlets.summary: April CPI data: food at home +0.7% in a single month — biggest jump since Aug 2022. Beef +14.8% YoY. Fruits and vegetables surging. War's economic toll on American families deepening.

outlets.name: MSNBC
outlets.headline: Israel is worried Trump will strike a 'bad deal' with Iran, potentially leaving nuclear program intact
outlets.summary: Israeli officials privately lobbying Washington against any ceasefire deal without full dismantlement of Iranian enrichment sites — putting Netanyahu and Trump on collision course.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump to Xi: 'We'll win with you or without you' — Departs for Beijing with Iran at top of agenda
outlets.summary: Fox News frames Trump's Beijing trip as a show of strength: US willing to win the Iran conflict unilaterally; China visit is diplomatic pressure, not dependence.

outlets.name: Breitbart
outlets.headline: Hezbollah Launches Record 105 Attacks on Israel — Most Intense Day of War
outlets.summary: Covering the Hezbollah attack record as evidence that the ceasefire framework is collapsing and Iran-backed proxies are emboldened by the fragile diplomatic situation.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war live: Trump arrives in Beijing as ceasefire hangs by a thread and Hezbollah escalates
outlets.summary: Live coverage: Trump-Xi summit opens as Iran rejects US proposal, Hezbollah launches record attacks, and Australia joins Hormuz naval mission. Araghchi heading to New Delhi for BRICS.

outlets.name: Reuters
outlets.headline: Oil at $107 as Iran rejects US peace framework; Trump-Xi summit seen as last diplomatic chance
outlets.summary: Oil prices reflect market skepticism about imminent Hormuz reopening. Analysts say only Chinese pressure on Tehran can break the deadlock.

outlets.name: BBC
outlets.headline: Pentagon confirms $29 billion spent on Iran war as Hegseth defends munitions stockpile before Senate
outlets.summary: Senate hearing reveals $29B cost with $1.5T FY27 defense budget request — the largest since WWII. Bipartisan questioning on weapons depletion and cost sustainability.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Hezbollah launches record 105 attacks on Israel — one killed in Nahariya, missile targets Ashdod Naval Base
outlets.summary: Comprehensive coverage of the most intense day of Hezbollah attacks since the war began. Times of Israel liveblog documenting all strike locations and casualties in real time.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Netanyahu vs. Trump: The widening rift over Iran deal terms as Beijing summit opens
outlets.summary: Netanyahu's CBS interview declaration that the war is 'not over' directly contradicts Trump's ceasefire push. 64% of Israeli Jewish citizens say ending war under current conditions contradicts security interests.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: US intel: Iran retains 70% of missile stockpile — raising questions about war's military success
outlets.summary: US intelligence finding that Iran kept 30 of 33 Hormuz missile sites operational sparks debate about whether Operation Epic Fury met its stated objectives.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran's strategic edge widens as Armed Forces issue new readiness directives; US approach 'main obstacle' to peace
outlets.summary: Iranian state media frames the situation as Iran holding the strategic upper hand: missile stockpile intact, Hormuz under Iranian control, US peace proposals rejected as 'surrender demands.'

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Araghchi heads to New Delhi BRICS summit as Iran works diplomatic track away from US pressure
outlets.summary: Iran International covers Araghchi's BRICS FM meeting trip as Tehran works to build non-Western diplomatic support ahead of resumed negotiations.
